title: A note on building reliability infrastructure for AI agents — and why post-incident debugging matters more than pre-flight validation.
summary: SafeRun is reliability infrastructure for AI agents that prioritizes post-incident debugging over pre-flight validation. The article traces the development roadmap from prototype to production, emphasizing how decision-time context snapshotting and replay capabilities enable teams to understand what went wrong. This approach matters for engineering teams shipping AI agents into production with real money and customer data at stake.
content:
A few weeks ago I started building SafeRun — inline reliability infrastructure for AI agents in production. The temptation, when you're building something in the agent reliability space, is to lead with validation. Block the bad action before it happens. Stop the runaway loop. Enforce the policy.
The failure mode no one talks about
Why observability tools don't solve this
The four-step loop, and why Replay is the foundation
The Stripe boolean problem
What we shipped, in order:
Phase 0: Working prototype with six failure simulations, including the Stripe boolean problem.
Phase 1: Persistent backend on Supabase. Replays survive page reload, browser close, account switch.
Phase 2: POST /v1/check-action API with sub-50ms p95 latency. Decision-time context snapshotting (inputs, retrieved context, external state, policy version, evaluator model version) captured synchronously, persisted asynchronously. The replay is built from the decision, not assembled after.
Phase 3: Python and TypeScript SDKs. Three-line install. @guard decorator wraps any tool call.
Phase 4: Intent Guard — catches valid-shape, wrong-intent tool calls. The Stripe boolean problem from above. Visible confidence scores, threshold calibration as a product surface, feedback loop closes back into recalibration.
Phase 5: Multi-tenant, project-scoped API keys, environment separation (dev logs, staging warns, production blocks), replay redaction, audit log, rule versioning.
Phase 6: Design partner onboarding, Prevention Impact Dashboard.
Phase 7: Self-hosted/VPC, SSO/SAML, audit log export, SOC 2 readiness, SafeRun as an MCP-callable tool.
The whole roadmap exists in service of the Replay layer. Every phase compounds on the previous one. Every feature ladders to Replay → Understand → Create Rule → Prevent.
What's next
We're onboarding the first design partners now. Engineering teams shipping AI agents into production — agents that move real money, modify real customer data, talk to real customers. Free during

zh.title: 关于为AI代理构建可靠性基础设施的说明 — 以及为什么事后调试比飞行前验证更重要
zh.summary: SafeRun是AI代理的可靠性基础设施，优先考虑事后调试而不是飞行前验证。该文章追踪了从原型到生产的开发路线图，强调了决策时刻的上下文快照和重放功能如何使团队能够理解出了什么问题。这种方法对于将AI代理部署到生产环境（涉及真实资金和客户数据）的工程团队很重要。

title: Equip: an open-source LMS for Bible schools — bilingual, scripture-aware, 4 weeks of real users
summary: Equip is a free, open-source LMS designed for small Bible schools and ministries, featuring bilingual Russian-English support with automatic translation and scripture-aware content handling. The platform launched in production with 14 users and three issued certificates, built to cost zero and be manageable by nontechnical administrators. This addresses a gap left by commercial LMSes that aren't designed for ministry-sized cohorts.
content:
TL;DR. Equip is a free, open-source LMS built for Bible schools and small ministries. MIT-licensed. Bilingual Russian↔English out of the box — the same course renders in either language depending on who's reading. Self-hostable end-to-end on free tiers. 4 weeks in production: 14 real users, 3 published courses, 3 issued certificates. This post is what's in it, what's wired in, and how it's built.

Small Bible schools, home-church training programs, and missionary-prep tracks around the world run their coursework on paper, WhatsApp groups, or shared Google Sheets. The commercial LMSes either price out small ministries (Canvas, Blackboard), require an IT department to run (Moodle, Open edX), or fight the use case at every step. Most are built for K–12 or universities, not 30 students reading through Romans together.
I wanted an LMS that:
Costs zero to run for a 20–100 student school
Has no vendor lock-in (your data, your hosting)
Handles scripture correctly — more on this below, it turned out to be the most interesting constraint
Works in Russian and English without manual per-string translation
Is small enough that one nontechnical ministry admin can run it end-to-end
That's Equip. Live at equipbible.com.
This is the feature that does the most work, so it goes first.
Russian and English UI ship together, but the more interesting decision is content. A teacher writes a course in their own language. When a student of the other language enrolls, every text field on every entity (course name, module title, chapter content, quiz question, quiz option, quiz explanation, assignment prompt) is auto-translated via Gemini Flash Lite and cached per (entity_type, entity_id, field, locale). The translation runs once per field and reads from cache thereafter. Cost per course is measured in cents.
The author always sees their own source-language content. The student always sees their target-language content. No "switch language to see English" toggle. No leaking source-locale strin

zh.title: Equip：为圣经学校设计的开源学习管理系统 — 双语、圣经感知、4周真实用户
zh.summary: Equip是一个免费的开源学习管理系统，专为小型圣经学校和事工设计，具有双语俄英支持、自动翻译和圣经感知内容处理功能。该平台以14个用户和三份已颁发证书的形式在生产中推出，构建成零成本且由非技术管理员管理。这解决了商业学习管理系统留下的空白，这些系统不是为事工规模的队列设计的。

title: Am I the only .NET dev who keeps building workflow engines on top of Hangfire?
summary: A .NET developer discusses the pattern of building workflow engines on top of Hangfire to handle complex multi-step processes like user signup that require sequential steps, parallel execution, delays, and retry logic. While Hangfire is reliable for simple fire-and-forget jobs, real-world processes expose limitations: without orchestration, the business flow becomes hidden and failure states multiply. A dedicated workflow engine abstraction matters when background job execution needs to become stateful and observable.
content:
I’ve been building .NET apps for about seven years, and I keep running into the same problem.
For background jobs, my default answer has usually been Hangfire.
And to be clear: I like Hangfire.
No shade at all. It is simple, reliable, and boring in the best way.
BackgroundJob.Enqueue(() => SendWelcomeEmail(userId));

Point it at SQL Server or Postgres, enqueue the job, ship it, go home.
But every time I need something more than fire-and-forget, I end up doing the same thing: slowly building a workflow engine on top of it.
Badly.
A pretty common example: user signup.
A user signs up and the system needs to:
create the user
charge the card
provision a tenant
send a welcome email
add them to the CRM
wait 24 hours
check whether they activated
send a nudge if they did not
The first version is usually just one big handler.
await users.Create(...);
await stripe.Charge(...);
await tenants.Provision(...);
await email.SendWelcome(...);
await crm.Add(...);

It works in dev. It demos well. Everyone is happy.
Then reality shows up.
Stripe is slow. The CRM returns 500s. SendGrid rate-limits you. A deploy happens halfway through. Now you have users in every possible half-finished state:
paid, but no tenant
tenant created, but no email
email sent, but no CRM record
CRM record created, but activation nudge never scheduled
So you do the reasonable .NET thing: split it into jobs.
BackgroundJob.Enqueue(() => ProvisionTenant(userId));
BackgroundJob.Enqueue(() => SendWelcomeEmail(userId));
BackgroundJob.Enqueue(() => AddToCrm(userId));

That is better.
But then the next set of problems starts.
Once the flow is split into jobs, the business process becomes harder to see.
A job can fail. A continuation can run. A delayed job can be scheduled. But the system does not really understand the whole flow.
What I usually want is something like:
run step A
if step A succeeds, run B and C in parallel
wait for both
pause for 24 hours
resume later
wait for an external event, but only up to 7 days
ret

zh.title: 我是唯一一个在Hangfire之上构建工作流引擎的.NET开发者吗？
zh.summary: 一位.NET开发者讨论了在Hangfire之上构建工作流引擎的模式，以处理复杂的多步骤流程，如用户注册，需要顺序步骤、并行执行、延迟和重试逻辑。虽然Hangfire对于简单的即发即忘工作可靠，但现实世界的流程暴露了局限性：没有编排，业务流程变得隐藏，故障状态增加。当后台工作执行需要变成有状态和可观察时，专门的工作流引擎抽象很重要。

title: Istio 1.30 Deep Dive — Agentgateway, Ambient Multicluster, TrafficExtension API, and 4 CVE Patches (JWKS RSA Leak, XDS Debug Auth)
summary: Istio 1.30 introduces Agentgateway, a new data-plane proxy designed for AI agent and MCP server traffic, alongside major Ambient mode improvements like CIDR ServiceEntry support and Tokio runtime metrics. The release consolidates Wasm and Lua extensibility under a unified TrafficExtension API while patching four critical CVEs including JWKS RSA key leaks and unauthenticated XDS debug endpoint access. This release doubles normal quarterly scope and positions Istio as infrastructure for the AI era.
content:
Istio 1.30 Deep Dive — Agentgateway, Ambient Multicluster, TrafficExtension API, and 4 CVE Patches (JWKS RSA Leak, XDS Debug Auth)


On May 18, 2026, the Istio community shipped Istio 1.30.0 alongside backports 1.29.3 and 1.28.7. On the surface it's a regular quarterly release, but the content is roughly double the normal scope. First, Agentgateway — a new Gateway API data-plane proxy built for AI agent and MCP server traffic — is wired in as an experimental GatewayClass, replacing Envoy on the gateway pod when enabled. Second, Ambient mode finally crosses an operability threshold with CIDR ServiceEntry, optional XFCC synthesis at waypoints, configurable HBONE window sizing, and Tokio runtime metrics in ztunnel. Third, the new TrafficExtension API lands as a unified replacement for WasmPlugin, consolidating Wasm and Lua extensibility behind a single resource that applies to sidecars, gateways, and waypoints. And decisively — four security advisories are patched together: CVE-2026-31837 (JWKS fallback leaks an RSA private key, enabling JWT forgery), CVE-2026-31838 (XDS debug endpoints on plaintext port 15010 reachable without authentication), CVE-2026-39350 (regex metacharacters in AuthorizationPolicy SPIFFE/namespace fields are not escaped), and CVE-2026-41413 (JWKS URI CIDR blocking is bypassed via DNS redirects and issuer discovery). This article decomposes the 1.29 → 1.30 breaking changes — XDS debug auth becoming mandatory, CNI config permissions tightening to 0600, and the sidecar service-namespace selection order flipping from alphabetical to "Kubernetes Service first" — across the five components that matter (ztunnel, waypoint, CNI, istiod, istioctl), and walks through the upgrade checklist we ran on a lab cluster (EKS 1.33 + Ambient + multi-network).
Istio promoted Ambient mode to beta in 1.24 (2024) and graduated Ambient Multicluster to alpha in late 2025. At KubeCon EU 2026, CNCF framed Istio as a "future-ready service mesh for the AI era" — the three flag

zh.title: Istio 1.30 深度解析——Agentgateway、环境多集群、TrafficExtension API 和 4 个 CVE 补丁（JWKS RSA 泄露、XDS 调试认证）
zh.summary: Istio 1.30 引入了 Agentgateway，这是一个为 AI 代理和 MCP 服务器流量设计的新数据平面代理，同时推出了重大的 Ambient 模式改进，如 CIDR ServiceEntry 支持和 Tokio 运行时指标。该版本在统一的 TrafficExtension API 下整合了 Wasm 和 Lua 可扩展性，同时修复了四个关键 CVE，包括 JWKS RSA 密钥泄露和未经身份验证的 XDS 调试端点访问。此版本将季度正常范围扩大了一倍，并将 Istio 定位为 AI 时代的基础设施。

title: Adding live sync to a Notion finance template without Zapier, Make, or a backend published: false
summary: WealthOS Pro extends a Notion finance template with local-first live sync for stock prices, SIP dates, and alerts using a small Node.js app that runs on the user's machine, eliminating the need for Zapier or Make. The architecture keeps all data in the user's Notion workspace while providing a no-terminal, double-click launcher designed for non-developers. This balances feature completeness with simplicity by moving sync logic to the user's device.
content:
How I turned WealthOS Lite into WealthOS Pro: a local-first Notion portfolio tracker with double-click sync for prices, SIP dates, and alerts.


tags: notion, javascript, productivity, showdev
A few weeks ago I wrote about shipping WealthOS Lite: a free Notion finance template that intentionally did less.
Two databases. A few formulas. No scripts. No API keys. No setup ceremony.
That post was about restraint.
This one is about the thing I deliberately left out of Lite:
live sync.
Most Notion finance templates run into the same wall eventually.
Manual tracking is clean, but stale.
Automation is useful, but the usual answer is:
connect Make.com
connect Zapier
pay monthly
create a bunch of scenarios
hope the integration does not break
That did not feel right for a personal finance system.
So for the Pro version, I tried a different architecture:
Keep Notion as the interface.

Keep the data in the user's workspace.

Run the sync locally on the user's own machine.
No hosted backend.

No Zapier.

No Make.

No subscription automation layer.
Just Notion + a small local Node.js sync app.
I wanted WealthOS Pro to support a more complete portfolio:
stocks
mutual funds
ETFs
fixed deposits
gold
government bonds
retirement accounts
share plans
real estate
insurance
loans
financial goals
tax savings
alerts and reminders
But I did not want to turn it into a SaaS product.
That meant no central server storing user data.
The sync app had to run locally.
The user flow needed to be simple enough for non-developers:
1. Download ZIP
2. Duplicate Notion template
3. Double-click launch.command or launch.bat
4. Complete browser setup
5. Click Run Sync
No terminal commands.

That last part mattered more than I expected.

As developers, we are very comfortable saying:

node wealthos-sync.mjs
For normal users, that is already too much.

So the real product was not the script.

The product was making the script disappear.

The architecture
At a high level, WealthOS Pro looks like this:

WealthOS

zh.title: 为 Notion 财务模板添加实时同步，无需 Zapier、Make 或后端
zh.summary: WealthOS Pro 使用在用户机器上运行的小型 Node.js 应用程序扩展 Notion 财务模板，为股票价格、SIP 日期和警报提供本地优先的实时同步，无需 Zapier 或 Make。该架构将所有数据保持在用户的 Notion 工作区中，同时提供了为非开发人员设计的无终端、双击启动器。通过将同步逻辑移到用户的设备上，这平衡了功能完整性和简洁性。

title: Google I/O 2026 AI Roundup: Every Feature You Actually Need to Know
summary: Google I/O 2026 shipped Gemini 3.5 Flash—40% cheaper and 2x faster than prior versions with genuine multimodal reasoning—making it the rational default for cost-sensitive production workloads and RAG pipelines. Project Astra, the always-on AI assistant, graduated from research to production, while AI Mode in Search gives Gemini agents autonomous live search execution and fact verification. These releases advance agent autonomy and cost-efficiency while introducing new failure modes like drift from hallucinated search results.
content:
Google I/O 2026 was a two-hour AI barrage. Gemini 3.5 Flash, Search AI Mode, Project Astra in Workspace, and a fleet of coding agents. If you blinked, you missed half of it. Here is the practical breakdown — no fluff, just what ships, when it ships, and what it actually means for people building with it.
The headline upgrade. Gemini 3.5 Flash is Google's new everyday model — 40% cheaper than 3.0 Flash, 2x faster on long-context tasks, and the first Flash-tier model with genuine multimodal reasoning across text, images, audio, and video. It ships with a 1M-token context window and a new Mixture-of-Experts architecture that routes queries to specialized sub-models.
The practical takeaway: if you were using Gemini 2.5 Flash for cost-sensitive production workloads, 3.5 Flash is a drop-in upgrade. The pricing alone (roughly half the cost per million tokens) makes it the rational default for agent loops, batch inference, and retrieval-augmented generation pipelines.
Google quietly launched "AI Mode" in Search, and then — more significantly — made it available inside Gemini as a tool. The model can now execute live Google searches autonomously, parse results, and synthesize answers without a human in the loop. This is the same capability OpenAI shipped with ChatGPT Browse, but Google's version has direct access to the Knowledge Graph and indexed web at scale.
For developers, it means Gemini agents can now self-verify facts, pull real-time pricing, and resolve obscure API documentation without you pre-loading context. It also introduces a new failure mode: agent drift from hallucinated search results. Google claims a 97% factual accuracy rate in internal testing, which is high — but not zero.
Project Astra, Google's always-on camera-and-screen AI assistant, is graduating from research preview to production. Starting Q3 2026, it will be available in Google Workspace as a persistent sidebar that can see your screen, read your documents, and execute multi-step actions across G

zh.title: Google I/O 2026 AI 综述：每个你实际需要了解的功能
zh.summary: Google I/O 2026 推出了 Gemini 3.5 Flash——比先前版本便宜 40%，速度快 2 倍，具有真正的多模态推理——使其成为对成本敏感的生产工作负载和 RAG 管道的合理默认选择。Project Astra 这个始终在线的 AI 助手从研究阶段晋升到生产阶段，而 Search 中的 AI 模式为 Gemini 代理提供了自主实时搜索执行和事实验证。这些版本推进了代理自主性和成本效率，同时引入了新的故障模式，如幻觉搜索结果的漂移。

title: Why I Stopped Writing 15 * 60 * 1000 in Every Project
summary: flowcap is a framework-agnostic rate-limiting library that eliminates millisecond calculations (15 * 60 * 1000) by accepting human-readable duration strings like '15m' instead. The library works across Express, Fastify, Koa, and vanilla Node.js with a universal middleware signature, solving the friction that each framework typically requires a different package with different APIs. This quality-of-life improvement compounds across thousands of projects.

zh.title: 为什么我停止在每个项目中写 15 * 60 * 1000
zh.summary: flowcap 是一个框架无关的速率限制库，通过接受像 '15m' 这样的人类可读持续时间字符串来消除毫秒计算（15 * 60 * 1000）。该库可在 Express、Fastify、Koa 和纯 Node.js 中使用，具有通用中间件签名，解决了每个框架通常需要不同的包和不同 API 的摩擦。这种生活质量的改进在数千个项目中产生复合效应。

title: AI Tesla FSDWaymo
summary: Autonomous driving is transitioning from modular pipelines (perception→planning→control) to end-to-end deep learning approaches, with Tesla FSD V12 demonstrating the viability of pure vision-based solutions while Waymo dominates robotaxi deployment. The article examines four architectural approaches—traditional modular systems, pure end-to-end, hybrid rule-backed end-to-end, and multimodal LLM-based approaches—comparing tradeoffs in interpretability, data requirements, and safety. By 2026, end-to-end deep learning has become industry consensus across major automakers.
content:
AI 自动驾驶与端到端深度学习完全指南：Tesla FSD、Waymo、端到端方案实战



  
  
  前言


自动驾驶正在从"模块化"走向"端到端"。Tesla FSD V12 证明了纯视觉端到端方案的可行性，Waymo 则在 Robotaxi 领域持续深耕。2026年，端到端自动驾驶已经成为行业共识。

自动驾驶技术架构对比：

1. 模块化方案 (传统)

- 感知 → 规划 → 控制

- 优点：可解释性强、易调试

- 限制：误差累积、规则复杂

- 代表：大多数传统车企

2. 端到端方案 (Tesla FSD V12)

- 传感器输入 → 驾驶输出

- 优点：无误差累积、类人驾驶

- 限制：可解释性差、需大量数据

- 代表：Tesla、Wayve

- 端到端 + 规则兜底

- 优点：平衡安全与性能

- 代表：华为、小鹏

4. 多模态大模型方案

- VLM + 驾驶策略

- 优点：泛化能力强

- 代表：DriveGPT、UniAD



import torch

import torch.nn as nn

from typing import Dict, List, Tuple, Optional

import numpy as np

class OccupancyNetwork:

"""占用网络 (Occupancy Network)"""

def __init__(self, voxel_size: float = 0.1):

self.voxel_size = voxel_size

self.encoder = None

self.decoder = None

def build_encoder(self, input_channels: int = 12):

"""构建编码器"""

self.encoder = nn.Sequential(

nn.Conv3d(input_channels, 64, kernel_size=3, padding=1),

nn.BatchNorm3d(64),

nn.ReLU(inplace=True),

nn.MaxPool3d(2),

nn.Conv3d(64, 128, kernel_size=3, padding=1),

nn.BatchNorm3d(128),

nn.ReLU(inplace=True),

nn.MaxPool3d(2),

nn.Conv3d(128, 256, kernel_size=3, padding=1),

nn.BatchNorm3d(256),

nn.ReLU(inplace=True),

def voxelize(

points: np.ndarray,

grid_range: Tuple[float, float, float] = (-50, 50, -50, 50, -5, 5)

) -> torch.Tensor:

points: (N, 3) 点云

grid_range: (x_min, x_max, y_min, y_max, z_min, z_max)

x_min, x_max, y_min, y_max, z_min, z_max = grid_range

voxel_x = ((points[:, 0] - x_min) / self.voxel_size).astype(int)

voxel_y = ((points[:, 1] - y_min) / self.voxel_size).astype(int)

voxel_z = ((points[:, 2] - z_min) / self.voxel_size).astype(int)

(voxel_x >= 0) & (voxel_x < int((x_max - x_min) / self.voxel_size)) &

(voxel_y >= 0) & (voxel_y < int((y_max - y_min) / self.voxel_size)) &

(voxel_z >= 0) & (voxel_z < int((z_max - z_min) / self.voxel_size))

return points[valid], voxel_x[valid], voxel_y[valid], voxel_z[valid]

def forward(self, points: np.ndarray) -> Dict:

valid_points, vx, vy, vz = self.voxelize(points)

grid_size = (

int(10

zh.title: AI 特斯拉 FSD 和 Waymo
zh.summary: 自动驾驶正在从模块化流程（感知→规划→控制）过渡到端到端深度学习方法，特斯拉 FSD V12 展示了纯视觉解决方案的可行性，而 Waymo 在无人出租车部署中占据主导地位。该文章检查了四种架构方法——传统模块化系统、纯端到端、混合规则支持的端到端和多模态 LLM 方法——比较了可解释性、数据需求和安全性的权衡。到 2026 年，端到端深度学习已成为主要汽车制造商的行业共识。

title: Claude Code Hooks: Security Gates for Agent Workflows
summary: Claude Code hooks attach scripts to lifecycle events in agent workflows, providing deterministic guardrails that execute automatically rather than relying on LLM instructions alone. The feature enables three categories of gates: safety gates (blocking risky commands), quality gates (formatting and validation), and operational glue (notifications and audit logs), all defined in reviewable project settings. This narrowness principle—each hook handling one concrete rule—makes hooks practical for production workflows where agents operate across real repositories.
content:
Claude Code hooks turn agent preferences into deterministic workflow gates. Instead of asking an LLM to remember "do not run risky shell commands" or "format files after edits," you can attach scripts to lifecycle events and make the rule execute every time the event fires.
That matters because coding agents are now operating inside real repositories. They can read files, propose shell commands, edit source, spawn subagents, and work across long sessions. Soft instructions still help, but the strongest guardrails live outside the model: small scripts, narrow matchers, explicit exit codes, and reviewable settings.
Effloow Lab ran a local sandbox PoC for this article. The sandbox used simulated Claude Code hook JSON payloads, not a live interactive /hooks session. It verified two production-shaped patterns: a PreToolUse Bash guard that blocks a risky pipe-to-shell command, and a PostToolUse formatter that runs Prettier after a file write. The evidence note is saved at data/lab-runs/claude-code-hooks-production-dev-workflow-guide-2026.md.
Claude Code already has permissions, project instructions, subagents, skills, and MCP integration. Hooks occupy a different layer. According to the official hooks guide, hooks run automatically at specific lifecycle points so repetitive rules happen deterministically instead of relying on the model to choose them.
That makes hooks useful for three categories of developer workflow:
Safety gates: block suspicious Bash commands, protected file writes, secret reads, or unsafe deployment steps.
Quality gates: format changed files, run type checks, add generated context after compaction, or validate configuration changes.
Operational glue: send notifications, record audit logs, load environment context, or react when the working directory changes.
The important design principle is narrowness. A good hook handles one concrete rule. It does not become a second build system, a hidden deployment script, or a pile of unreviewed shell logic.
The

zh.title: Claude Code 钩子：代理工作流的安全门
zh.summary: Claude Code 钩子将脚本附加到代理工作流中的生命周期事件，提供自动执行的确定性防护栏，而不仅依赖 LLM 指令。该功能启用三类门：安全门（阻止危险命令）、质量门（格式化和验证）和操作粘合剂（通知和审计日志），全部在可审查的项目设置中定义。这种狭隘原则——每个钩子处理一个具体规则——使钩子对于代理在真实存储库中操作的生产工作流切实可行。

title: The Bane of International Payment Systems for Open Source Developers in Sub-Saharan Africa
summary: OpenAid, an open-source platform helping nonprofits allocate funds in sub-Saharan Africa, discovered that international payment platforms like Gumroad, PayPal, and Stripe have no presence in Nigeria, limiting contributor payment options. The team eventually integrated a Nigerian bank supporting IBANs, revealing a systemic problem: only two of seven Nigerian banks supported international account numbers at the time. This highlights infrastructure gaps that disproportionately affect open-source contributors outside North America and Western Europe.
content:
The Problem We Were Actually Solving


The project 'OpenAid' is an OSS platform to help non-profits collect and allocate funds more efficiently to grassroots projects in sub-Saharan Africa. We decided to allow contributors to receive funding through the platform in the form of bank transfers. This was to help reduce transaction fees that come with traditional payment methods like PayPal and Stripe. At first, it seemed like a simple problem to solve but little did I know that I would be venturing into a rabbit hole of international payment systems.
We initially decided to use Gumroad and Payhip to handle transactions, but it turned out that these platforms did not have a presence in Nigeria. I didn't think much of this at first, assuming that it was just an issue with the platforms since PayPal and Stripe did not work either. However, we soon realized that it was not our problem to solve but rather the platforms we were using. Contributors were getting frustrated with the lack of payment options available, which in turn affected the overall morale of the project. This was when we started to look for alternative solutions that would work in Nigeria.
We decided to integrate the Flatforma platform, which is an open-source platform that simplifies the process of accepting donations and payments from the public, with bank transfer options available for Nigeria. However, this was not without its challenges. One of the biggest issues we faced was the need for international bank account numbers (IBANs) from Nigerian banks that could be easily accessed by the platform. After some research, we discovered that only 2 out of 7 banks in Nigeria supported IBANs. This made it difficult to find a bank that met our requirements. In the end, we decided to go with a bank that supported IBANs and used their API to handle transactions. 
After integrating the Flatforma platform and finding a bank that supported IBANs, we saw a significant increase in payment success rates. We went from ha

zh.title: 撒哈拉以南非洲开源开发者面临的国际支付系统困境
zh.summary: OpenAid是一个帮助非营利组织在撒哈拉以南非洲分配资金的开源平台。该平台发现Gumroad、PayPal和Stripe等国际支付平台在尼日利亚没有业务，限制了贡献者的支付选项。团队最终整合了一家支持IBAN的尼日利亚银行，揭示了一个系统性问题：在当时，七家尼日利亚银行中只有两家支持国际账户号码。这突出了基础设施差距，这些差距对北美和西欧以外的开源贡献者产生了不成比例的影响。

title: Serverless Contact Form — Lambda, API Gateway, DynamoDB, and SES
summary: A fully serverless contact form uses API Gateway, Lambda, DynamoDB, and SES to handle submissions with effectively zero operational cost and no server management. The deployment uses AWS SAM which simplifies infrastructure definitions compared to plain CloudFormation, but requires careful attention: HttpApi events must reference AWS::Serverless::HttpApi, not the native ApiGatewayV2::Api resource, or SAM silently skips route creation. This pattern demonstrates building stateless HTTP endpoints with minimal infrastructure overhead.
content:
Overview


The contact form on bonefishsoftware.com is fully serverless — no EC2, no always-on server. A visitor submits the form, the request hits an API Gateway HTTP API, a Python Lambda function validates and stores the submission in DynamoDB, then sends an email notification via SES. Cost at low volume: effectively zero.

The contact API is deployed using AWS SAM (Serverless Application Model), a CloudFormation extension that simplifies Lambda + API Gateway resource definitions.
Why SAM over plain CloudFormation?

SAM's AWS::Serverless::Function with Events automatically creates the API Gateway routes, integrations, Lambda permissions, and stage. Doing this in plain CloudFormation requires 6–8 separate resource definitions. SAM condenses it to one function resource with an Events section.
AWS::Serverless::HttpApi vs AWS::ApiGatewayV2::Api


The most important thing to get right: when using SAM's HttpApi event type, the ApiId must reference an AWS::Serverless::HttpApi resource — not a native AWS::ApiGatewayV2::Api.
Using the wrong resource type results in the SAM transform silently skipping route and integration creation, leaving you with a deployed API that returns 404 on every request.
# ✅ Correct — SAM manages routes/integrations automatically
ContactApi:
  Type: AWS::Serverless::HttpApi
  Properties:
    CorsConfiguration:
      AllowOrigins: [https://bonefishsoftware.com]
      AllowMethods: [POST, OPTIONS]
      AllowHeaders: [Content-Type]

ContactFunction:
  Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
  Events:
    PostContact:
      Type: HttpApi
      Properties:
        ApiId: !Ref ContactApi   # ← references Serverless::HttpApi
        Path: /contact
        Method: POST

# ❌ Wrong — routes NOT created by SAM
ContactApi:
  Type: AWS::ApiGatewayV2::Api  # ← native resource, SAM ignores events

# lambda/contact/handler.py

import json, os, uuid
from datetime import datetime, timezone
import boto3
from botocore.exceptions import ClientError

ALLOWED_ORIGIN = os.envi

zh.title: 无服务器联系表单——Lambda、API Gateway、DynamoDB和SES
zh.summary: 完全无服务器的联系表单使用API Gateway、Lambda、DynamoDB和SES来处理提交，运营成本几乎为零，无需服务器管理。部署使用AWS SAM，相比纯CloudFormation简化了基础设施定义，但需要仔细注意：HttpApi事件必须引用AWS::Serverless::HttpApi，而不是本地ApiGatewayV2::Api资源，否则SAM会静默跳过路由创建。这个模式演示了如何用最少的基础设施开销构建无状态HTTP端点。

title: CI/CD with AWS CodePipeline and CodeBuild
summary: A GitHub-to-S3-to-CloudFront pipeline using AWS CodePipeline and CodeBuild automates React app deployments with every push to main, eliminating manual steps through IaC defined in CloudFormation. The setup requires CodeStar Connections to authenticate GitHub and the AWS Connector GitHub App, with one mandatory manual authorization step in the AWS Console that cannot be automated. This pattern demonstrates continuous deployment from source control through build, artifact storage, and CDN invalidation.
content:
Overview


Every push to the main branch on GitHub automatically builds the React app and deploys it to S3 + CloudFront — zero manual steps. This article covers how that pipeline is wired together using AWS CodePipeline, CodeBuild, and GitHub via CodeStar Connections.

The pipeline infrastructure is defined in infra/stacks/pipeline.yml.
GitHubConnection:
  Type: AWS::CodeStarConnections::Connection
  Properties:
    ConnectionName: bonefish-github
    ProviderType: GitHub

Manual step required: After deploying the stack, you must go to AWS Console → CodePipeline → Settings → Connections and click "Update pending connection" to authorize the GitHub App. This cannot be automated — AWS requires explicit human approval to grant access to your GitHub account.
When authorizing, install the AWS Connector for GitHub app on your GitHub account and grant it access to the specific repository. "Connect as a GitHub user" only works for CodeBuild — CodePipeline requires the GitHub App.
Intermediate pipeline artifacts (source zip, build output) are stored in a private S3 bucket:
ArtifactBucket:
  Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
  Properties:
    BucketName: !Sub 'bonefish-pipeline-artifacts-${AWS::AccountId}'
    VersioningConfiguration:
      Status: Enabled
    PublicAccessBlockConfiguration:
      BlockPublicAcls: true
      BlockPublicPolicy: true
      IgnorePublicAcls: true
      RestrictPublicBuckets: true

BuildProject:
  Type: AWS::CodeBuild::Project
  Properties:
    Name: bonefish-build
    ServiceRole: !GetAtt CodeBuildRole.Arn
    Artifacts:
      Type: CODEPIPELINE
    Environment:
      Type: LINUX_CONTAINER
      ComputeType: BUILD_GENERAL1_SMALL
      Image: aws/codebuild/standard:7.0
      EnvironmentVariables:
        - Name: S3_BUCKET
          Value: !Ref S3BucketName
        - Name: DISTRIBUTION_ID
          Value: !Ref DistributionId
    Source:
      Type: CODEPIPELINE
      BuildSpec: buildspec.yml

Pipeline:
  Type: AWS::CodePipeline::Pipeline
  Properties:
    Nam

zh.title: 使用AWS CodePipeline和CodeBuild实现CI/CD
zh.summary: 使用AWS CodePipeline和CodeBuild的GitHub-S3-CloudFront管道通过每次对main分支的推送自动化React应用部署，通过CloudFormation中定义的基础设施即代码消除了手动步骤。该设置需要CodeStar Connections来认证GitHub和AWS Connector GitHub应用，需要在AWS控制台中进行一个无法自动化的强制手动授权步骤。这个模式演示了从源代码控制、构建、工件存储到CDN失效的连续部署。
